name: Papermill (Parameterized Jupyter Notebook Execution)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3); nteract; Netflix open source

auth.notes: Local execution tool; S3/GCS/Azure storage backends available

performance.notes: Execution time depends on notebook content

agent_readiness.notes: Executes Jupyter notebooks with parameters. CLI: papermill input.ipynb output.ipynb -p alpha 0.6 -p ratio 0.1. Python: import papermill as pm. pm.execute_notebook('template.ipynb', 'output.ipynb', parameters={'alpha': 0.6}). Tag cells as 'parameters'. S3/GCS/Azure storage: papermill s3://bucket/in.ipynb s3://bucket/out.ipynb. Progress display. Error handling with retries. Used at Netflix for parametrized reporting. Integrates with Airflow, Prefect. Enables notebook as a job pattern.

gotchas: Notebook execution state can be fragile if kernel crashes
Parameters cell must be tagged in Jupyter interface
Large notebooks can be slow to serialize output
Not a replacement for proper DAG orchestration
best_when: Parameterized report generation from Jupyter notebooks
Notebook as a job pattern in ML pipelines
Data science pipelines where notebooks are the artifact
avoid_when: When Python scripts would serve better (notebooks add overhead)
summary: Papermill executes parameterized Jupyter notebooks. pm.execute_notebook('template.ipynb', 'output.ipynb', parameters={'alpha': 0.6}). CLI: papermill input.ipynb output.ipynb -p alpha 0.6. Tag cells with 'parameters' in Jupyter. S3/GCS/Azure storage support. Netflix's tool for parameterized notebook reporting. Enables 'notebook as a job' pattern. Integrates with Airflow and Prefect.
